title: Scale NATS to 3-node cluster with JetStream persistence
subtasks: Update NATS Helm values or manifest to set cluster size to 3 nodes (replicas: 3).
Enable JetStream persistence in the NATS configuration, specifying a persistent volume claim for data durability.
Configure NATS cluster with appropriate cluster name and peer discovery.
Set resource requests/limits for production (e.g., 256Mi–512Mi RAM, 250m–500m CPU per pod).
Validate cluster formation: all 3 nodes join and are visible in the NATS monitoring endpoint.
Test JetStream: create a stream, publish and consume messages, verify persistence across pod restarts.

title: Configure TLS certificates and Ingress for NATS and PM server
subtasks: Install or verify cert-manager is present in the cluster.
Create or reference a ClusterIssuer for Let's Encrypt (production endpoint).
Update or create Ingress resources for the PM server API (e.g., api.sigma1.5dlabs.io) with TLS enabled and cert-manager annotations.
If NATS is exposed externally (for cross-region or external clients), create a secure Ingress with TLS for NATS client connections.
Validate certificate issuance: ensure Ingress endpoints serve valid TLS certificates trusted by browsers.
Document Ingress hostnames and TLS secret names in the deployment README.

title: Configure Ingress controller and CDN for frontend with cache headers
subtasks: Update or create Ingress resource for the frontend (e.g., sigma1.5dlabs.io) with TLS via cert-manager.
Add CDN cache-control headers for static assets (e.g., Cache-Control: public, max-age=3600) using ingress annotations or configuration snippets.
Integrate with Cloudflare CDN: ensure DNS for sigma1.5dlabs.io is proxied through Cloudflare and cache rules are set for static asset paths.
Test CDN caching: deploy a static asset, fetch via curl, and verify Cache-Control header and Cloudflare cache status.
Document CDN configuration and cache header policy in deployment docs.

title: Enforce network policies for pod-to-pod and egress traffic
subtasks: Create a default-deny NetworkPolicy for the production namespace.
Allow frontend pods to connect only to the PM server on port 3000.
Allow PM server pods egress to required external APIs (Linear, GitHub, Discord, Nous) and DNS.
Block all other pod-to-pod and egress traffic by default.
Test enforcement: from a frontend pod, verify only PM server is reachable; from PM server, verify only allowed external endpoints are accessible.
Document network policy rules and test results.

title: Deploy OpenTelemetry Collector and Jaeger with persistent storage
subtasks: Deploy OpenTelemetry Collector as a Deployment or DaemonSet in the production namespace.
Configure sampling rates appropriate for production (e.g., 1–10% for traces, 100% for errors).
Deploy Jaeger (all-in-one or production mode) with a PersistentVolumeClaim for trace storage.
Update PM server and frontend deployments to export traces to the OpenTelemetry Collector endpoint.
Validate trace ingestion: generate sample traffic and confirm traces appear in Jaeger UI.
Document trace retention policy and access instructions for Jaeger.

title: Configure alert rules for SLO violations and critical errors
subtasks: Define SLOs for PM server and frontend (e.g., 99.9% availability, <1s p95 latency).
Create PrometheusRule or Alertmanager configuration for SLO violation alerts (e.g., high error rate, high latency, pod restarts).
Integrate alert notifications with existing channels (e.g., Slack, PagerDuty, or email).
Test alert firing: simulate an SLO breach and verify alert delivery.
Document SLO definitions and alerting policy.
